step_by_step_reasoning: Data is replicated from on-premise SQL Server to an S3 landing bucket using AWS DMS. Landing updates trigger S3 event notifications to SQS queues. Amazon EventBridge schedules run AWS Step Functions to orchestrate AWS Glue ETL jobs. Glue consumes queue events to update the S3 Delta Lake. Data Scientists then query the Delta Lake for machine learning models.

graph.name: True Blue Serverless Data Lake Architecture
graph.notes: Serverless data lake architecture utilizing AWS Glue to process database transactions and updates in near-real time instead of EMR.

nodes.name: Landing S3
nodes.notes: S3 landing bucket for raw transactional Parquet files.

nodes.name: Amazon SQS
nodes.notes: Buffers S3 event notifications categorized by latency.

nodes.name: Amazon EventBridge
nodes.notes: Triggers step functions based on scheduled latency rules.

nodes.name: AWS Glue
nodes.notes: Serverless ETL jobs processing and writing updates to Delta Lake.

nodes.name: Delta Lake S3
nodes.notes: Three-tiered (bronze, silver, gold) Delta Lake storage.

nodes.name: Data Scientist
nodes.notes: Data scientists querying Delta Lake tables via PySpark SQL.

edges.notes: Writes Parquet files to landing bucket.
S3 event notifications published to SQS.
Glue reads S3 file locations from queues.
Writes upserts and updates to Delta Lake.
Queries views and raw data using PySpark.
